Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Leo Kontonickas, et al.
Linda L. Nusser of GORDON & REES LLP (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / opposing party Siemens Industry Inc.
Erica J. Russell of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / opposing party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' AND DEFENDANT SIEMENS INDUSTRY INC.'S JOINT MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE AND MAINTAIN PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A)

Hearing held. The Court made the ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' and defendant Siemens Industry Inc.'s joint motion to continue trial date for good cause is granted for 60 days to December 10, 2012 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. The motion for preference pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) is re-granted. All discovery shall remain open until the trial date. Summary motions may be heard on 15 days notice without an additional two days for e-service. Last day for hearing summary motions is 12/6/2012, with oppositions due 5 court days before the hearing and replies due 2 court days before the hearing. Only the newly filed MSJ motions or CCP 473 motions can be heard. Case Management Conference set in Dept. 603 at 1:30pm on 11/14/2012. Plaintiffs will provide all medical records promptly to Berry & Berry. There will be a mandatory settlement conference on 12/10/2012 at 1:30 p.m. with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5. Parties to submit settlement conference statements by 12/6, 4:00 p.m. The statements will be kept confidential. All counsel attending the conference must have knowledge about the fact of the case, and be present with the representatives who have authority to settle. Any person(s) need to appear by phone must seek approval by the Court prior to the conference. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR # 8875 (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Sep-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeffrey T. Thayer of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leo Kontonickas, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 5915 (503/TLJ)

AMENDED MOTION For Order Severing Case From Group And Promptly Assigning Ou
Off Calendar Jun-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Chris Hersom of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties
Linda Nusser of GORDON REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / opposing party
Hans C. Ruschke of DE HAY ELLISTON (510-285-0742), appearing for defendant / opposing party

RULING:
Argued and the Court grants Plaintiff's Motion for Preference pursuant to CCP 36(a). Plaintiffs stipulate to waive the irregularities of preference and additional 15 days are added to the last day of preference. Last day of preference is extended from 10/3/12 to 10/18/12. The current trial date of 2/25/13 is vacated and this case is severed from Brayton Group 622. Trial date is 10/15/12 at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 503. Parties stipulated to the last day for hearing summary motions is 9/27/12 and that summary motions may be heard on 15 days notice without an additional two days for e-service. Oppositions are due five court days before the hearing and replies due two court days before the hearing. Plaintiff is to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than 9/7/12. Settlement conference is set in room 603 at 1:30pm on 9/27/12. Fact discovery cut-off date is 9/21/12. Expert discovery cut-off date is 10/5/12. No later than 7/13/12, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than 7/13/12, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference is set on 9/19/12 at 1:30pm. in Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. Mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 is set on 10/15/12 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 503. All clients are to be present unless permission is granted otherwise. Confidential statements are due by noon five days prior to the mandatory settlement conference. Written order pending. Judge: Teri L. Jackson; Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436, AIKEN WELCH COURT REPORTERS (510-451-1580) (503, TLJ)
